title: Malty Crumbly Saudi Arabian Wagyu Kebabs
description: A weeknight Saudi Arabian dish featuring Wagyu beef, cooked in a ttukbaegi earthenware pot and wrapped in pandan leaves, with a malty and crumbly texture, this recipe is gluten-free friendly.
instructions: Preheat the ttukbaegi earthenware pot over medium high heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together malt syrup, coconut oil, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Add the Wagyu beef cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the malt syrup mixture.
Thread the beef cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Wrap each skewer with a pandan leaf, securing with a toothpick if needed.
Place the skewers in the preheated ttukbaegi earthenware pot and cook for 10 minutes, turning occasionally.
Reduce heat to medium low and cook for an additional 10 minutes, or until the beef is cooked to desired doneness.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with additional pandan leaves if desired.
